Day 1 · instagram

Resume glow-up

Day 1: Show your resume before/after and explain the 3 fixes that made it ATS-friendly.

Day 2 · linkedin

ATS myth busting

ATS does not hate you. It just needs clear structure, relevant keywords, and plain language.

Day 3 · twitter

One-line resume tip

Replace vague bullets with impact: action + metric + outcome.

Day 4 · tiktok

Job search confidence

A quick pep talk on why rejection is data, not a verdict.

Day 5 · facebook

Cover letter hook

Share 3 opening lines that feel human, specific, and recruiter-friendly.

Day 6 · linkedin

Interview prep

Share the 5 interview questions every candidate should rehearse before a final round.

Resume glow-up

Day 1: Show your resume before/after and explain the 3 fixes that made it ATS-friendly.

ATS myth busting

ATS does not hate you. It just needs clear structure, relevant keywords, and plain language.

One-line resume tip

Replace vague bullets with impact: action + metric + outcome.

Job search confidence

A quick pep talk on why rejection is data, not a verdict.

Cover letter hook

Share 3 opening lines that feel human, specific, and recruiter-friendly.

Interview prep

Share the 5 interview questions every candidate should rehearse before a final round.

Day 7 · twitter

Networking DM

A simple outreach template that feels warm instead of awkward.

Day 8 · instagram

Portfolio checklist

The 4 sections a portfolio needs to actually help you land interviews.

Day 9 · linkedin

Career change story

Reframe your pivot as a strength: transferable skills + proof of momentum.

Day 10 · tiktok

Common resume mistake

Stop listing duties only. Hiring teams want outcomes, not chores.

Day 11 · instagram

Before/after bullet

Turn a weak resume bullet into one that sounds sharp, specific, and measurable.

Day 12 · linkedin

Job application strategy

Apply smarter: one tailored resume, one tailored cover note, one follow-up.

Day 13 · twitter

Salary negotiation

You can be polite and still ask for what you're worth.

Day 14 · tiktok

Recruiter mindset

What recruiters are actually scanning for in the first 10 seconds.

Day 15 · facebook

Career win

Celebrate a small win: better keyword match, cleaner layout, stronger summary.

Day 16 · linkedin

LinkedIn headline

A strong headline blends role, value, and niche in one clear line.

Day 17 · twitter

Bullet formula

Use this formula: verb + scope + metric + result.

Day 18 · tiktok

First 60 seconds

What to say when interviewers ask: 'Tell me about yourself.'

Day 19 · facebook

Resume summary

Your summary should sound like a confident elevator pitch, not a biography.

Day 20 · linkedin

Job search consistency

The job search rewards rhythm. Small daily actions beat random bursts.

Day 21 · threads

Follow-up email

Send the follow-up. Short, grateful, and specific always wins.

Day 22 · instagram

Skills section

Don't stuff your skills with fluff. Prioritize what the job description actually asks for.

Day 23 · linkedin

Action verbs

Swap weak wording for stronger action verbs to make experience feel decisive.

Day 24 · tiktok

Interview nerves

A grounding routine before interviews: breathe, review, and walk in prepared.

Day 25 · youtube

60-second resume walkthrough

Record a short walkthrough showing how ResumeVaultGod.com improves a real resume.

Day 26 · pinterest

Career cheat sheet

Create a pin with the top 5 resume mistakes and how to fix them fast.

Day 27 · linkedin

Personal branding

Your online presence should reinforce the story your resume tells.

Day 28 · twitter

Keyword alignment

The goal is not to trick ATS. The goal is to match the language of the role.

Day 29 · instagram

Interview answer

Record one strong answer today. Better answers come from rehearsal, not pressure.

Day 30 · reddit

30-day wrap-up

Close the month by reviewing what improved: resume, confidence, clarity, and consistency.
